question: How does Xinpinmu specifically solve Temu's tariff problem, and what does the full value chain look like?

findings.claim: US eliminated de minimis exemption for China/HK imports effective May 2, 2025, ending Temu's zero-tariff model
findings.data: Executive order signed April 2, 2025. All postal items from China/HK under $800 now subject to duty: initially 30% or $25/item (rising to $50 after June 1). Later escalated to $100/item (May 2) then $200/item (June 1). Post-trade deal (May 14, 2025): 54% tariff on formerly de-minimis goods, or $100 per postal parcel.

findings.claim: US eliminated de minimis for ALL countries effective August 29, 2025, with per-item duties based on IEEPA tariff rate brackets
findings.data: Global de minimis rates: $80/item for countries with <16% IEEPA rate, $160/item for 16-25%, $200/item for >25%. From February 28, 2026 onward, only ad valorem methodology (not per-item flat fee) may be used.

findings.claim: A $10 Temu item that cost $10 pre-tariff now costs $15.40-$24.50 depending on rate regime
findings.data: Pre-May 2025: $10 item = $10 final cost (0% tariff via de minimis). May 2025 at 120-145%: $10 item = $22-$24.50. Trade truce at 54%: $10 item = ~$15.40. Concrete example: CNBC reported a $18.47 dress became $44.68 after $26.21 import charges (142% surcharge). A $12.44 bathing suit became $31.12 (150% fee).

findings.claim: US Supreme Court ruled IEEPA does not authorize emergency tariffs (Feb 20, 2026), but de minimis suspension continues
findings.data: February 20, 2026: US Supreme Court ruled IEEPA does not authorize the emergency tariffs imposed in 2025. However, executive order continued suspension of duty-free de minimis treatment globally with revised duty rates.

findings.claim: New US law signed July 2025 permanently eliminates de minimis for all commercial shipments from all countries, effective July 1, 2027
findings.data: A new law signed in July 2025 will eliminate the de minimis exemption for all commercial shipments from all countries, effective July 1, 2027 - marking a permanent, legislative (not executive order) end to low-value import duty exemptions.
